onboarding.step3: Which frameworks do you want to track?

dashboard.healthGlance: Your business health at a glance
dashboard.activateHint: Activate a framework in Settings to start tracking health.

modes.title: Mode Library
modes.subtitle: 29 modes across 4 frameworks. Find the right workout for your business.
modes.activateHint: Activate a framework in Settings to see modes.

coach.title: Talk to Alicia
coach.subtitle: Your business coaching colleague
coach.greeting: Hey! What are we working on?
coach.greetingSub: I know your business. Ask me anything — strategy, validation, team, scaling, AI collaboration.

assessments.title: Assessment Center
assessments.subtitle: Scenario-based diagnostics. No self-ratings — real situations with real choices.
assessments.noScenarios: No scenarios available for this assessment.

progress.title: Progress
progress.subtitle: Your business fitness rings. Completion, consistency, growth.
progress.progressRingsAria: Progress rings showing completion, consistency, and growth

activity.title: Activity
activity.subtitle: Your business fitness timeline. Sessions, assessments, coaching.
activity.empty: No activity yet. Start a mode to see your timeline.

programs.title: Training Programs
programs.subtitle: Guided multi-mode sequences. Not "do whatever." Structured capability building.
programs.noActive: No active program. Browse the catalog to enroll.

teams.title: Teams
teams.subtitle: Collective health, shared progress, team-level coaching.
teams.emptyState: Create a team to start tracking collective health and progress.

settings.title: Settings
settings.name: Name
settings.frameworksHint: Toggle frameworks to unlock modes, health tracking, and coaching.
settings.deleteConfirm: This will permanently delete your account and all data. Are you sure?

core.name: Core X
core.description: Validation-driven business building. Test assumptions, capture insights, build propositions.

air.name: Air X
air.description: Async-first flex work. Information flow, team rhythms, remote culture.

max.name: Max X
max.description: Scaling without bureaucracy. Org structure, cross-team alignment, quality at scale.

synergy.name: Synergy X
synergy.description: Human-AI collaboration. Onboarding AI colleagues, trust calibration, decision protocols.

frameworkToggle.activeHint: Active — modes and health tracking enabled

errors.alreadyEnrolled: Already enrolled in an active program

validation.name: Validation Mode
validation.purpose: Test your riskiest business assumption with evidence and make a clear Persevere/Pivot/Experiment Again decision.
validation.trigger: Use when you are about to make a significant business decision based on something you believe but have not tested.
validation.doneSignal: Can you state your assumption, the evidence for or against it, and your decision in one sentence?

0.name: Assumption
0.description: The specific belief you are testing. Must be falsifiable.
0.example: Freelance designers will pay $49/month for automated invoicing.

1.name: Risk Level
1.description: How much damage if this assumption is wrong.

2.name: Current Evidence
2.description: What you already know. Data, conversations, signals.

3.name: Experiment Design
3.description: The cheapest, fastest test that could disprove the assumption.

4.name: Success Criteria
4.description: The specific number that means the assumption holds.

6.name: Results
6.description: What actually happened. Raw data, not interpretation.

7.name: Decision
7.description: Based on the evidence: Persevere, Pivot, or Experiment Again.

8.name: Next Action
8.description: The specific next step with a date.

9.name: Assumption Update
9.description: How has this assumption changed based on what you learned?

hooks.0: Assumptions identified in Business Engine feed into Validation
hooks.2: Experiment results feed into Insight Capture
hooks.3: Validated results can be packaged for team review

insight-capture.name: Insight Capture Mode
insight-capture.purpose: Turn raw signals from your market into structured patterns and actionable next steps.
insight-capture.trigger: Use when you have raw data, customer conversations, or market signals that need structure.
insight-capture.doneSignal: Can you explain this insight to someone who was not in the room, with enough context for them to act on it?

0.name: Signal Source
0.description: Where did this insight come from?

1.name: Raw Signal
1.description: The exact observation, quote, or data point.

2.name: Pattern Match
2.description: Does this connect to anything you have seen before?

3.name: Strength
3.description: How strong is this signal?

4.name: Implication
4.description: If this signal is real, what does it mean for your business?

5.name: Action
5.description: What specific next step does this insight drive?

hooks.0: Experiment results feed into Insight Capture

proposition-builder.name: Proposition Builder Mode
proposition-builder.purpose: Build evidence-backed value propositions in three tiers: Basic, Satisfier, Delighter.
proposition-builder.trigger: Use when you need to articulate what you offer and why it matters, grounded in evidence.
proposition-builder.doneSignal: Can a new team member explain your value proposition in 30 seconds, including who it is for and why they care?

0.name: Target Customer
0.description: Who exactly is this proposition for?

1.name: Problem Statement
1.description: The specific problem you are solving, in their words.

2.name: Basic Tier
2.description: The minimum viable value — what you must deliver.

3.name: Satisfier Tier
3.description: Expected value that meets the standard.

4.name: Delighter Tier
4.description: Unexpected value that creates loyalty.

5.name: Evidence
5.description: What validated data supports each tier?

6.name: Differentiation
6.description: What makes your proposition different from alternatives?

hooks.0: Validated insights inform propositions
hooks.1: Proposition feeds into business model design

business-engine.name: Business Engine Mode
business-engine.purpose: Map your business model assumptions and identify which ones could break the engine.
business-engine.trigger: Use when you need to understand how your business works as a system and which assumptions are load-bearing.
business-engine.doneSignal: Can you explain your business model in one sentence, name the riskiest assumption, and say what evidence you have for it?

0.name: Revenue Model
0.description: How does money come in? Be specific about pricing and channels.

1.name: Cost Structure
1.description: Where does money go? Fixed vs variable costs.

2.name: Key Assumptions
2.description: List the 3-5 assumptions your model depends on.

3.name: Riskiest Assumption
3.description: Which assumption, if wrong, breaks the model?

4.name: Current Evidence
4.description: What data supports or challenges each assumption?

5.name: Unit Economics
5.description: Revenue per unit, cost per unit, margin.

hooks.0: Assumptions feed into priority ranking

priority-stack.name: Priority Stack Mode
priority-stack.purpose: Create an evidence-based forced ranking of priorities with a clear Not Doing list.
priority-stack.trigger: Use when you have more things to do than time to do them, and need ruthless clarity on what matters.
priority-stack.doneSignal: Can you tell someone your top 3 priorities and your Not Doing list without hesitation?

0.name: Candidates
0.description: List everything competing for your attention right now.

1.name: Criteria
1.description: What criteria will you use to rank? Impact, urgency, evidence strength.

2.name: Forced Rank
2.description: Rank all candidates 1 to N. No ties allowed.

3.name: Top 3
3.description: Your top 3 priorities with one-sentence justification each.

4.name: Not Doing List
4.description: Everything below the cut line. Say it explicitly.

5.name: Time Allocation
5.description: How will your time split across the top 3 this period?

hooks.0: Assumptions from Business Engine need ranking
hooks.1: Top priorities feed into execution tracking

execution-tracker.name: Execution Tracker Mode
execution-tracker.purpose: Track work visibility and identify blockers with async-first status tracking.
execution-tracker.trigger: Use when you need to see what is happening, what is stuck, and what needs unblocking.
execution-tracker.doneSignal: Can you see all active work, know what is blocked, and have a specific unblock action for each blocker?

0.name: Work Items
0.description: List active work items connected to your priorities.

1.name: Status
1.description: For each item: Not Started, In Progress, Blocked, Done.

2.name: Blockers
2.description: What is blocked and why? Be specific.

3.name: Unblock Actions
3.description: For each blocker: who needs to do what by when?

4.name: Cycle Time
4.description: How long from start to completion for recent items?

hooks.0: Priorities define what to track
hooks.1: Completed items go to Delivery Check

delivery-check.name: Delivery Check Mode
delivery-check.purpose: Quality-gate completed work and monitor post-delivery performance.
delivery-check.trigger: Use when work is done and needs a quality check before calling it shipped.
delivery-check.doneSignal: Can you confidently say this deliverable is ready for the person who receives it?

0.name: Deliverable
0.description: What was delivered? Be specific.

1.name: Quality Criteria
1.description: What standards must it meet?

2.name: Quality Assessment
2.description: Does it meet each criterion? Pass/Fail per item.

3.name: Issues Found
3.description: What needs fixing before ship?

4.name: Post-Ship Monitoring
4.description: What will you watch after delivery to confirm quality?

hooks.0: Completed work items from Execution Tracker

information-architecture.name: Information Architecture Mode
information-architecture.purpose: Design where information lives so people can find what they need in 5 minutes without asking.
information-architecture.trigger: Use when people keep asking where things are, information is duplicated or lost, or new joiners cannot find what they need.
information-architecture.doneSignal: Can a new team member find any piece of critical information in 5 minutes without asking someone?

0.name: Information Audit
0.description: Where does information currently live? List all tools and locations.

1.name: Pain Points
1.description: What information is hardest to find? What questions get asked repeatedly?

2.name: Categories
2.description: Group information into logical categories.

3.name: Single Source of Truth
3.description: For each category, where is the canonical location?

4.name: Findability Test
4.description: Can a new team member find X in 5 minutes? Test 3 common items.

hooks.0: Information structure enables async work design
hooks.1: Information categories feed AI knowledge structure

flex-work-design.name: Flex Work Design Mode
flex-work-design.purpose: Define async defaults, sync exceptions, meeting budget, and energy rhythms for your team.
flex-work-design.trigger: Use when your team is spending too much time in meetings, working across time zones, or struggling with work-life boundaries.
flex-work-design.doneSignal: Can every team member explain when to default to async, when sync is appropriate, and what their meeting budget is?

0.name: Current Meeting Load
0.description: How many hours per week does each person spend in meetings?

1.name: Async Default
1.description: What is your default: async or sync? Define the rule.

2.name: Sync Exceptions
2.description: What specifically requires real-time interaction?

3.name: Meeting Budget
3.description: Maximum meeting hours per person per week.

4.name: Energy Rhythms
4.description: When are people most productive? How do you protect deep work?

5.name: Time Zone Protocol
5.description: How do you handle async across zones? Core overlap hours?

hooks.0: Information structure enables flex work
hooks.1: Work design feeds into team cadence

team-rhythm.name: Team Rhythm Mode
team-rhythm.purpose: Design your weekly cadence: sync touchpoints, deep work blocks, energy checkpoints.
team-rhythm.trigger: Use when the team lacks a consistent cadence, people do not know when to expect updates, or deep work keeps getting interrupted.
team-rhythm.doneSignal: Does every team member know the weekly rhythm without checking a calendar?

0.name: Weekly Cadence
0.description: Map out the recurring weekly rhythm for the team.

1.name: Sync Touchpoints
1.description: List the essential sync moments and their purpose.

2.name: Deep Work Blocks
2.description: When are protected focus periods? How are they enforced?

3.name: Energy Checkpoints
3.description: How do you check team energy and workload?

hooks.0: Work design defines rhythm constraints
hooks.1: Rhythm defines when decisions happen async vs sync

async-decision.name: Async Decision Mode
async-decision.purpose: Make a clear decision asynchronously with structured question, options, deadline, and dissent protocol.
async-decision.trigger: Use when a decision needs to be made and a meeting is not the best way to make it.
async-decision.doneSignal: Is the decision recorded, rationale documented, and all stakeholders notified?

0.name: Decision Question
0.description: The specific question being decided. One sentence.

1.name: Options
1.description: The options being considered, with pros/cons for each.

2.name: Decision Maker
2.description: Who makes the final call?

3.name: Deadline
3.description: When must the decision be made?

4.name: Input Requested From
4.description: Who needs to weigh in before the deadline?

5.name: Dissent Protocol
5.description: How can someone disagree? What happens with objections?

6.name: Decision Record
6.description: The final decision, rationale, and who was consulted.

hooks.0: Decisions should be packaged for team visibility

package.name: Package Mode
package.purpose: Create self-contained communication packages that do not require follow-up questions.
package.trigger: Use when sharing work, updates, or decisions with people who were not in the room.
package.doneSignal: Can someone act on this package without asking a single clarifying question?

0.name: Context
0.description: What does the reader need to know to understand this?

1.name: Key Points
1.description: The 3-5 most important things. Numbered.

2.name: Decision Needed
2.description: Is a decision needed? From whom? By when?

3.name: Deadline
3.description: When does the reader need to respond?

4.name: Feedback Format
4.description: How should the reader respond? Comment, approve, edit?

hooks.0: Validation results can be packaged

contribution-tracker.name: Contribution Tracker Mode
contribution-tracker.purpose: Make invisible work visible across 5 contribution types.
contribution-tracker.trigger: Use when some team members do critical work that nobody sees, or recognition is skewed toward visible deliverables.
contribution-tracker.doneSignal: Can you see contributions across all 5 types, not just delivery?

0.name: Team Member
0.description: Who are you tracking contributions for?

1.name: Delivery
1.description: Tangible outputs shipped.

2.name: Mentoring
2.description: Helping others grow, pairing, reviewing, teaching.

3.name: Documentation
3.description: Writing that helps others work independently.

4.name: Process Improvement
4.description: Making systems, tools, or workflows better.

culture-at-distance.name: Culture at Distance Mode
culture-at-distance.purpose: Define observable behaviors, rituals, conflict protocol, and trust signals for distributed teams.
culture-at-distance.trigger: Use when culture feels thin in a distributed team — people are productive but disconnected.
culture-at-distance.doneSignal: Could a new remote hire understand your team culture from documentation alone?

0.name: Observable Behaviors
0.description: What behaviors define your culture? List 5 that are visible in async work.

1.name: Rituals
1.description: Regular practices that reinforce culture. Not meetings — rituals.

2.name: Conflict Protocol
2.description: How do you handle disagreements when you cannot read body language?

3.name: Trust Signals
3.description: What builds trust in your distributed team? What erodes it?

4.name: Anti-Patterns
4.description: What behaviors are you actively trying to prevent?

dashboard.name: Dashboard Mode
dashboard.purpose: Set up dual metrics: performance (resolution time, cycle time) and dynamics (learning, collaboration, fun).
dashboard.trigger: Use when scaling past 50 people and traditional metrics miss the human side of the organization.
dashboard.doneSignal: Can you see both performance and dynamics at a glance, and know when to act?

0.name: Performance Metrics
0.description: Key operational metrics: cycle time, resolution time, lead time.

1.name: Dynamics Metrics
1.description: Human metrics: learning rate, collaboration score, commitment, fun.

2.name: Data Sources
2.description: Where does each metric come from?

3.name: Review Cadence
3.description: How often are these reviewed and by whom?

4.name: Action Triggers
4.description: What metric levels trigger action?

hooks.0: Dashboard signals feed into org structure review

org-map.name: Org Map Mode
org-map.purpose: Create a bird's-eye view of your organization: team types, gaps, and overlaps.
org-map.trigger: Use when the organization is growing and you need to see the whole picture.
org-map.doneSignal: Can you explain your org structure to a new VP in 5 minutes?

0.name: Teams
0.description: List all teams with type: Mission, Platform, or Leadership Circle.

1.name: Team Sizes
1.description: Head count per team.

2.name: Gaps
2.description: What capabilities are missing?

3.name: Overlaps
3.description: Where are teams duplicating effort?

hooks.0: Org map identifies teams needing blueprints

team-blueprint.name: Team Blueprint Mode
team-blueprint.purpose: Define mission, members, decision authority, cadence, and quality standards for a team.
team-blueprint.trigger: Use when creating a new team or when an existing team lacks clarity on its purpose and authority.
team-blueprint.doneSignal: Can every team member state the mission, their role, and what decisions they can make?

0.name: Team Mission
0.description: Why does this team exist? One sentence.

1.name: Members
1.description: Who is on the team and what are their roles?

2.name: Decision Authority
2.description: What decisions can this team make without escalation?

3.name: Cadence
3.description: How does the team communicate and coordinate?

4.name: Quality Standards
4.description: What quality bar does this team maintain?

hooks.0: Team blueprints define nodes in the connection map

connection-map.name: Connection Map Mode
connection-map.purpose: Map team interactions: smooth, friction, or broken. Find bottlenecks.
connection-map.trigger: Use when cross-team coordination is slow, handoffs drop, or teams blame each other.
connection-map.doneSignal: Can you show someone a map of all team connections with quality ratings and bottleneck fixes?

0.name: Team Pairs
0.description: List all team-to-team interaction pairs.

1.name: Interaction Quality
1.description: For each pair: Smooth, Friction, or Broken.

2.name: Bottlenecks
2.description: Which team pairs are the biggest bottlenecks?

3.name: Root Causes
3.description: Why is each bottleneck stuck?

4.name: Fix Actions
4.description: What specific action will improve each bottleneck?

hooks.0: Blueprints define the teams to map

relationship-health.name: Relationship Health Mode
relationship-health.purpose: Diagnose and fix inter-team friction with structured analysis.
relationship-health.trigger: Use when two teams have a Friction or Broken connection that needs repair.
relationship-health.doneSignal: Have both teams agreed on the fix protocol and committed to a review date?

0.name: Teams Involved
0.description: Which two teams have the friction?

1.name: Pain Generators
1.description: What specific actions or inactions cause pain?

2.name: Expectations Gap
2.description: What does each team expect from the other? Where do expectations differ?

3.name: Value Proposition
3.description: What value does each team provide to the other?

4.name: Fix Protocol
4.description: Agreed actions to improve the relationship.

hooks.0: Problem connections identified in Connection Map

company-priority.name: Company Priority Mode
company-priority.purpose: Align 3-5 company priorities with evidence, team translation, and dependency mapping.
company-priority.trigger: Use when the company has too many priorities, teams are pulling in different directions, or quarterly planning is needed.
company-priority.doneSignal: Can every team lead explain company priorities and how their team contributes?

0.name: Company Priorities
0.description: List 3-5 company priorities with evidence basis.

1.name: Team Translation
1.description: What does each priority mean for each team?

2.name: Dependencies
2.description: Which priorities depend on which teams?

3.name: Conflict Resolution
3.description: When priorities conflict, which wins?

hooks.0: Priorities drive execution tracking at scale

scaled-execution.name: Scaled Execution Mode
scaled-execution.purpose: Track cross-team execution with priority connections, key deliverables, and dependencies.
scaled-execution.trigger: Use when multiple teams are working toward the same priorities and need visibility into each other's progress.
scaled-execution.doneSignal: Can you see all cross-team work, dependencies, and blockers in one view?

0.name: Priority Connection
0.description: Which company priority does this work connect to?

1.name: Key Deliverables
1.description: What must be delivered and by which team?

2.name: Cross-Team Dependencies
2.description: Which teams depend on which deliverables?

3.name: Status
3.description: Current status of each deliverable.

4.name: Blockers
4.description: Cross-team blockers and unblock actions.

hooks.0: Priorities define what to track at scale

quality-matrix.name: Quality Matrix Mode
quality-matrix.purpose: Define consistent quality standards across teams: company-wide plus team-specific.
quality-matrix.trigger: Use when quality varies across teams and there is no shared definition of "good enough."
quality-matrix.doneSignal: Can any team member look up the quality standard for their work and know if they meet it?

0.name: Company-Wide Standards
0.description: Quality standards that apply to every team.

1.name: Team-Specific Standards
1.description: Standards unique to each team based on their domain.

2.name: Measurement
2.description: How is quality measured for each standard?

3.name: Review Process
3.description: Who reviews quality and how often?

ai-onboarding.name: AI Colleague Onboarding Mode
ai-onboarding.purpose: Define AI colleague roles, knowledge sources, access boundaries, and success metrics.
ai-onboarding.trigger: Use when starting to work with AI tools and you need to set up the collaboration properly.
ai-onboarding.doneSignal: Could you hand this onboarding document to a new team member and they would know exactly how your team uses AI?

0.name: AI Role
0.description: What role does the AI play in your workflow?

1.name: Knowledge Sources
1.description: What information does the AI need access to?

2.name: Access Boundaries
2.description: What should the AI NOT have access to?

3.name: Success Metrics
3.description: How will you measure if the AI collaboration is working?

4.name: Review Cadence
4.description: How often will you review the AI collaboration setup?

5.name: Escalation Triggers
5.description: When should a human take over from the AI?

hooks.0: AI setup feeds into task delegation assessment

centaur-assessment.name: Centaur Assessment Mode
centaur-assessment.purpose: Map every task: human-led, AI-led, or centaur (both) with capability assessment.
centaur-assessment.trigger: Use when you need to decide which tasks AI can handle and which need human judgment.
centaur-assessment.doneSignal: Can you explain for every recurring task who does it (human, AI, or both) and why?

0.name: Task Inventory
0.description: List all recurring tasks in your workflow.

1.name: Classification
1.description: For each task: Human-Led, AI-Led, or Centaur.

2.name: Capability Assessment
2.description: For AI/Centaur tasks: what is the AI good at and where does it need help?

3.name: Evolution Path
3.description: Which Human-Led tasks could become Centaur over time?

hooks.0: AI setup informs task delegation

decision-protocol.name: Decision Protocol Mode
decision-protocol.purpose: Define when AI leads decisions vs humans, override conditions, and retrospective cadence.
decision-protocol.trigger: Use when AI is involved in decisions and you need clear rules about authority and override.
decision-protocol.doneSignal: For any decision type, can you instantly say who has authority and what triggers an override?

0.name: Decision Categories
0.description: List the types of decisions in your workflow.

1.name: Authority Map
1.description: For each category: AI leads, Human leads, or Shared.

2.name: Override Conditions
2.description: When can a human override an AI decision? When can AI override a human recommendation?

3.name: Retrospective Cadence
3.description: How often do you review decision quality?

hooks.0: Delegation map informs decision authority

verification-ritual.name: Verification Ritual Mode
verification-ritual.purpose: Build risk-scaled review protocols: high-stakes gets deep review, low-stakes flows free.
verification-ritual.trigger: Use when AI output quality varies and you need consistent verification without slowing everything down.
verification-ritual.doneSignal: Can your team instantly classify any AI output by risk level and know the review protocol?

0.name: Output Categories
0.description: List the types of AI output your team produces.

1.name: Risk Level
1.description: For each category: High, Medium, or Low risk.

2.name: Review Protocol
2.description: For each risk level: what review is required?

3.name: Quality Threshold
3.description: What acceptance rate triggers a review of the protocol?

knowledge-architecture.name: Knowledge Architecture Mode
knowledge-architecture.purpose: Structure persistent AI context so your AI colleague does not ask the same questions every session.
knowledge-architecture.trigger: Use when AI keeps asking for context it should already have, or gives answers that ignore your business specifics.
knowledge-architecture.doneSignal: Can your AI colleague start a new session and immediately have the context it needs to be useful?

0.name: Knowledge Domains
0.description: What categories of knowledge does the AI need?

1.name: Persistent Context
1.description: For each domain: what should the AI always know?

2.name: Update Triggers
2.description: When should each knowledge domain be updated?

3.name: Access Pattern
3.description: How does the AI access this knowledge? Files, APIs, prompts?

hooks.0: Information structure feeds AI knowledge

trust-calibration.name: Trust Calibration Mode
trust-calibration.purpose: Set trust levels based on evidence, not feeling. Calibrate over-trust and under-trust.
trust-calibration.trigger: Use when you suspect the team either trusts AI too much or too little, and decisions are being made on gut feel rather than data.
trust-calibration.doneSignal: Can you justify every trust level with evidence, not intuition?

0.name: Trust Inventory
0.description: For each AI use case: current trust level (Over-trust, Calibrated, Under-trust, Untested).

1.name: Evidence
1.description: What data supports each trust level?

2.name: Calibration Actions
2.description: For over-trust: add verification. For under-trust: run a pilot.

3.name: Review Schedule
3.description: When will you re-calibrate trust levels?

ai-scaling.name: AI Collaboration Scaling Mode
ai-scaling.purpose: Plan how human-AI collaboration evolves as the organization grows.
ai-scaling.trigger: Use when AI collaboration is working for one person or team and needs to scale to the organization.
ai-scaling.doneSignal: Can you present a roadmap for AI collaboration that any team lead can follow?

0.name: Current State
0.description: How does AI collaboration work today? Who uses it, for what?

1.name: Scaling Path
1.description: How should AI collaboration evolve? Solo → Team → Org.

2.name: Standardization vs Customization
2.description: What should be standard across teams vs customized per team?

3.name: Infrastructure Needs
3.description: What tools, access, or training is needed to scale?

4.name: Risk Assessment
4.description: What could go wrong as you scale AI collaboration?

your-first-validation-week.name: Your First Validation Week
your-first-validation-week.description: Test your riskiest assumption in 5 days. Five modes, one evidence-based decision.

schedule.0: Map your business model. Identify 3 key assumptions.
schedule.1: Rank your assumptions by risk. Pick the riskiest one.
schedule.2: Design and launch a test for your riskiest assumption.
schedule.3: While waiting for results, capture signals from your market.
schedule.4: Review results. Make your Persevere/Pivot/Experiment Again decision.

async-maturity-sprint.name: Async Maturity Sprint
async-maturity-sprint.description: Cut meeting hours, build async decision-making muscle, and package work for your distributed team.

schedule.0: Map your current meeting cadence. Identify meeting bloat.
schedule.2: Package your next team update using the format.
schedule.3: Convert one upcoming meeting-based decision to async.
schedule.4: Package the async decision result. Measure clarity.
schedule.6: Set async-first defaults for your team.
schedule.7: Address the cultural gaps in your distributed setup.

scaling-readiness-check.name: Scaling Readiness Check
scaling-readiness-check.description: Prepare your organization for 50+ people. Structure, connections, priorities, execution.

schedule.1: Define blueprints for your key teams.
schedule.5: Translate company priorities to team level.

ai-colleague-bootcamp.name: AI Colleague Bootcamp
ai-colleague-bootcamp.description: Set up human-AI collaboration properly. Context, delegation, verification, trust.

schedule.0: Load your business context into your AI colleague.
schedule.1: Map your tasks: human-led, AI-led, centaur.
schedule.2: Define which decisions AI can make alone.
schedule.3: Build a protocol for checking AI output quality.
schedule.4: Structure what AI needs to know.
schedule.5: Set trust levels based on evidence, not feeling.
schedule.6: Plan how to scale what is working.

full-business-fitness.name: Full Business Fitness
full-business-fitness.description: The complete 7 Flows experience. 15 modes across all four frameworks in 6 weeks.

schedule.6: Make your first structured async decision.
